name: Flo's White Chocolate Raspberry Brownie Petit
recipeIngredient: 3 oz Unsalted Butter, cut into pieces
1/2 cup Fresh Raspberries, at room temperature
2 oz Unsalted Butter, cut into pieces
1/2 cup Fresh Red Raspberries, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (163°C). Line an 8-inch square baking pan with double thickness of aluminum foil extending 2 inches beyond two opposite sides. Fold the overhang down to form handles. Butter the bottom of the foil-lined pan.
2. Sift flour, baking soda, and salt onto a sheet of wax paper.
3. In a medium saucepan over low heat, melt butter. Add chocolate and let stand for 1 minute; whisk until smooth. Pour into a large bowl and let stand for 5 minutes until tepid. Whisk in sugars and corn syrup. Whisk in eggs, then vanilla. Stir in flour mixture until just blended. Carefully fold in raspberries.
4. Spread batter evenly into prepared pan and bake for 25 to 30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted 1 inch from the edge comes out with a moist crumb. Cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes. Invert onto the rack, carefully peel off foil, and cool completely.
5. Melt butter in the top of a double boiler over hot (not simmering) water. Transfer to a small bowl and let stand until tepid and slightly thickened. Spread half of the topping onto the brownie and refrigerate for 5 minutes until partially set. Fold remaining raspberries into the topping, divide among brownies, arrange attractively on top of each square, and loosely cover with plastic wrap. Refrigerate for 30 minutes to set topping completely. Cut into 16 squares, transfer to a serving plate, and cover tightly with plastic wrap. Brownies can be made up to 1 day ahead, refrigerated, and covered with plastic wrap. Bring to room temperature before serving.
